label: Sanity CMS for Next.js
summary: Use when a Next.js App Router site should read structured content from Sanity, with optional draft preview / Presentation Tool Visual Editing. Ships only frontend integration glue — env config, a lazy public+draft client factory, a query helper, and paired draft-mode enable/disable routes. When Sanity is not configured, Sanity-backed pages must render static example content plus SanityConfigNotice instead of crashing.

envVars.purpose: Sanity project ID that identifies which project to read content from. Find it in sanity.io/manage or the project's sanity.config.ts. Tagged feature-runtime because isSanityConfigured() gates all Sanity access after the promotion fixes: pages fall back to static example content + SanityConfigNotice, and the draft-mode routes answer 503 instead of crashing.
Sanity dataset to query, usually 'production'. Find it in the Sanity project's dataset settings. Same feature-runtime fallback as NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID.

Pinned Sanity API version date (e.g. 2025-09-25) for stable query behavior across Sanity API changes. Optional — the client falls back to a pinned default when unset, so an empty value is not even warning-worthy.

URL where the separate Sanity Studio is hosted; used for stega edit-intent links and the Presentation Tool. Optional — defaults to the local Studio dev URL, only relevant once draft/editing flows are wired up.

Server-only Sanity token with read access, needed for draft-mode preview and any private dataset. Create it in the Sanity project's API settings (Manage -> API -> Tokens). Tagged feature-runtime: without it the draft client throws internally, but the draft-mode enable route catches that via isSanityDraftTokenConfigured() and answers 503 instead of crashing; published-content reads keep working.

notes: Legacy import Fas D (2026-07-08): promoted from the sanity-cms-alt prospect draft, not the sanity-cms-primary one, after comparing both against dossiers-prospect/normalization-report.json (open decision #2 in docs/plans/active/2026-07-08-dossier-legacy-import.md) — alt shipped a paired draft-mode disable route + a sanityFetch() query helper that the primary draft lacked, and alt's exposes correctly named the actual exports (client/projectId/dataset) where the primary draft's exposes referenced nonexistent symbol names (sanityClient/sanityProjectId/sanityDataset). Applied fixes beyond the REVIEW.md list: dropped the module-level assertValue() throw in api.ts (now non-throwing + isSanityConfigured()), split getSanityClient()/getDraftSanityClient() (public CDN client without a token vs. server-only draft client with the token + useCdn:false), added isSanityDraftTokenConfigured() so the enable route degrades to 503 instead of throwing, and replaced the alt draft's disable route (which imported a disableDraftMode export that next-sanity/draft-mode does not actually ship) with the standard manual next/headers pattern. lastVerified carries the legacy source's scrape date (raw/cms-sanity-next-js-clean-app/skiss.json _importedAt) until the first preview/generate-test selects this dossier (no-false-green, same rule as PR #422/#430/#445).
